project_goal: AIOS — AI-native Linux distribution. Implement the spec contracts from rev.2 (53 sub-specs CONTRACT-grade) into working Rust + Python + KDE code, following §22 MVP golden path discipline.

New server.rs (~330 LoC, 4 inline unit tests): PolicyKernelService struct (Arc<dyn PolicyKernel> + engine_id + code_version + active_bundle_version + default_subject + started_at); DEFAULT_ENGINE_ID / DEFAULT_CODE_VERSION / DEFAULT_BUNDLE_VERSION constants. check_schema_version() accepts empty or canonical aios.policy.v1alpha1; mismatch → FailedPrecondition. build_context() anchors EnrichmentSnapshot.snapshot_id on env.request.request_hash() — until T-024 wires real AIOS-FS enrichment-snapshot ids, the request hash is the determinism anchor's first component so triple-collapsing maintains §13. evaluate_internal() drives the kernel for both EvaluatePolicy + SimulatePolicy (simulated arg flips PolicyDecision.simulated). impl proto::policy_kernel_server::PolicyKernel: EvaluatePolicy + SimulatePolicy + GetPolicyEngineInfo REAL; LoadBundle / RollbackBundle / ExplainDecision return Status::unimplemented with explicit T-024/T-025 deferral message. build_router(svc) + serve(svc, addr) bootstrap helpers. _T021_carryforward_note: T-021 closed — Subject hydration + AI self-approval prevention (S2.3 §7 + §17).
current_step_T021: T-021 landed. New crates/aios-policy/src/subject_hydration.rs (~300 LoC, 3 inline unit tests). SubjectHydrator async trait (single method hydrate(&self, provisional: &str) -> Result<HydratedSubject, PolicyError>) + InMemoryHydrator HashMap<String, HydratedRecord>-backed impl. HydratedRecord {subject, valid_until: Option<DateTime<Utc>>, revoked: bool} with chainable builder helpers HydratedRecord::new / .revoked() / .with_expiry(t). InMemoryHydrator::with_fixtures() pre-loads 4 canonical subject classes: human:lucky (Human, is_ai=false), agent:dev (Agent, is_ai=true — anchor for §17 fixtures), application:planner (Application, is_ai=true), service:systemd (Service, is_ai=false). Unknown / revoked / past-expiry records all collapse to PolicyError::SubjectUnauthenticated per §7 (the three failure modes deliberately not discriminated at the boundary). Blanket impl async_trait for Arc<T: SubjectHydrator + ?Sized + Send + Sync> so kernel can hold the hydrator behind Arc<dyn ...> and clone freely. Pipeline §3 step 2 wired: new DecisionPipeline::evaluate_with_chain(envelope, original_context, hydrated_context: Option<&PolicyContext>, engine) — hydrated_context = None signals SubjectUnauthenticated and the driver emits Decision::Deny + reason_code=SubjectUnauthenticated using the ORIGINAL context's bundle_version/enrichment_snapshot_id (so the rejected decision remains auditable). Old evaluate_with_hard_deny_engine becomes a thin wrapper passing Some(context). Kernel InMemoryPolicyKernel gains subject_hydrator: Option<Arc<dyn SubjectHydrator + Send + Sync>> field + 2 ctors: new_with_full_chain(hydrator, engine) + new_with_subject_hydrator(hydrator) (engine None). Existing new() + new_with_hard_deny(engine) ctors unchanged (T-017/T-018 baseline preserved). has_subject_hydrator() inspector helper. Dropped #[derive(Debug)] on InMemoryPolicyKernel (dyn trait field is not Debug); custom impl Debug prints `subject_hydrator: <bool>` instead — only loses the trait-object's Debug rendering, every other field is preserved. Kernel evaluate_policy now awaits hydrator.hydrate(envelope.identity.subject_canonical_id) BEFORE the sync pipeline driver, clones the original context, replaces .subject with the hydrated record, and passes the (Option<PolicyContext>) into evaluate_with_chain. Pipeline §17 evaluator landed as pure function evaluate_ai_self_approval_prevention(decision, subject, envelope) -> Option<ApprovalRequirement> + DecisionPipeline::apply_step_8(decision, subject, envelope) -> PolicyDecision that mints the upgrade. §17.1 risk-flag detection over envelope.request.target.risk.<flag> (destructive/privileged/network_exposure/secret_access/recovery_path_affected — 5 closed flags); §17.3 carve-out: all-flags-false AI subject keeps ALLOW unchanged. Upgrade preserves policy_decision_id + request_hash + bundle_version + enrichment_snapshot_id + evaluated_at; mutates decision → RequireApproval, reason_code → AiSelfApprovalUpgrade, reason_message → English §17 explanation, approval → {required:true, ExactRequestHash, ttl 300, approver_classes:[Human], require_human_co_signer:false}; rules_consulted bumps by 1 (saturating). new reason_code constants: SUBJECT_UNAUTHENTICATED, AI_SELF_APPROVAL_UPGRADE. Pipeline driver's step_8_ai_self_approval_prevention() remains a Continue no-op because the step-7 scoped-allow stub means no ALLOW partial state reaches step 8 on the end-to-end path until T-022's rule index lands; apply_step_8 is the public hook tests + future T-022 call. lib.rs re-exports: SubjectHydrator, InMemoryHydrator, HydratedRecord, evaluate_ai_self_approval_prevention. New tests/subject_hydration.rs (8 tests): fixtures_provide_the_four_canonical_subject_classes / hydration_is_deterministic_for_the_same_provisional_id / unknown_provisional_id_rejects_with_subject_unauthenticated / expired_record_rejects_with_subject_unauthenticated / dyn_subject_hydrator_through_arc_is_usable / kernel_with_hydrator_short_circuits_unknown_subject_to_subject_unauthenticated / kernel_with_hydrator_replaces_placeholder_with_hydrated_subject (verifies hydration runs + DefaultDeny floor still fires after success) / kernel_without_hydrator_uses_passed_subject_unchanged (backward compat T-017/T-018). New tests/ai_self_approval.rs (8 tests): ai_subject_with_privileged_action_upgrades_allow_to_require_approval / human_subject_with_privileged_action_remains_allow / ai_subject_with_deny_decision_remains_deny (§17.2 never downgrades) / ai_subject_with_no_risk_flags_keeps_allow_per_section_17_3 (§17.3 carve-out) / upgraded_decision_carries_human_approver_class (+ ExactRequestHash + required:true) / pure_evaluator_returns_some_only_for_ai_allow_with_risk (full truth-table for Decision × subject.is_ai × risk_flag) / upgrade_preserves_original_identity_fields_for_audit (decision_id + request_hash + bundle_version + snapshot id preserved + reason_code changes + rules_consulted+1) / application_subject_also_triggers_section_17_upgrade. Total +19 (3 inline + 8 + 8). Workspace 479 → 498. All 4 gates: cargo check + test (498) + clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings + fmt --all -- --check ALL PASS. _T022_carryforward_note: T-022 closed — Policy bundle loader (S2.3 §12, signed + version-pinned).
current_step_T022: T-022 landed. New crates/aios-policy/src/bundle.rs (~250 LoC). PolicyBundle envelope per S2.3 §12: bundle_version (polb_<hex> content-addressed), bundle_id (operator label), created_at (DateTime<Utc>), signing_authority (trust-store key), signature_ed25519 (Vec<u8>, base64-STANDARD on JSON wire via private `mod base64_bytes` serde codec), rules (Vec<PolicyRule>). PolicyRule per §11.2 proto shape: rule_id, scope (RuleScope), effect (RuleEffect closed enum {Unspecified, Allow, Deny}), priority (i32, default 0), subjects (Vec<String>), actions (Vec<String>), conditions (Vec<String> — multiple §9.1 predicates ANDed per spec, NOT single string as brief implied), constraints (Option<Constraints>), approval (Option<ApprovalRequirement>), reason_code (String). RuleScope closed enum: Global / PerSubjectType / PerActionTarget / PerSubject / PerAction (orthogonal routing-hint over §5 ladder). SignedBundleBody<'a> view omits signature_ed25519 — canonical body bytes for Ed25519 sign+verify; bundle.canonical_signed_body_bytes() returns serde_json deterministic output. #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] on both PolicyBundle + PolicyRule. New crates/aios-policy/src/bundle_loader.rs (~190 LoC). BundleLoader struct holds HashMap<String, VerifyingKey> trust store + Option<expected_bundle_version>. load_from_bytes(&[u8]) -> Result<PolicyBundle, PolicyError> runs 5 ordered checks: (1) serde_json parse → InvalidPolicyBundle(JSON deserialise: ...); (2) version pin compare → BundleVersionMismatch{expected,found}; (3) authority lookup → BundleUnknownAuthority(name); (4) Ed25519 verify over canonical_signed_body_bytes → BundleSignatureInvalid; (5) per-rule conditions_parser::parse + RuleEffect::Unspecified rejection → InvalidPolicyBundle(rule <id> condition: <err>). load_from_file(&Path) wraps std::fs::read. Constructors: new(trust)/with_version_pin(trust, version)/pin_version/clear_version_pin/expected_bundle_version/trusts_authority. PolicyError +4 variants: InvalidPolicyBundle(String) / BundleSignatureInvalid / BundleVersionMismatch{expected,found} / BundleUnknownAuthority(String) — all with canonical Display strings. Dep additions: aios-policy Cargo.toml +ed25519-dalek (workspace 2.1) +blake3 (workspace) +base64 0.22; dev-dep rand_core (workspace 0.6). Workspace Cargo.toml +base64 0.22 entry. lib.rs re-exports PolicyBundle, PolicyRule, RuleEffect, RuleScope, BundleLoader. Tests tests/bundle.rs (~280 LoC, 15 tests): happy round-trip, malformed JSON, per-rule condition parse failure (or operator triggers DisallowedToken), perturbed-body bad signature (replace human:lucky→human:alice without re-signing), unknown authority (empty trust store), version pin mismatch, version pin match (positive), empty rules, determinism (parse-twice byte-equal + re-serialise round-trip), positive signature material round-trip, RuleScope serde all 5 variants, 50-rule scale smoke, PolicyError Display strings, RuleEffect::Unspecified rejection, load_from_file. TRUST SPEC applied — rule keeps `conditions: Vec<String>`; scope kept as orthogonal routing-hint enum with the brief's three values + PerSubject + PerAction (additions consistent with §5 tier matching). RuleEffect::Unspecified rejection is an extra invariant beyond the brief — proto3 zero-value sentinel must not round-trip through the loader (mirrors Decision::Unspecified treatment in §4). Closed AST: Condition (conjunction of predicates per §9.1 AND-only EBNF — no Or, no Not, no parens variants, per spec deviation from brief — documented in module header), Predicate (Compare / In / Contains / Exists), CompareOp (Eq/Neq/Lt/Lte/Gt/Gte), Namespace (6 closed: Subject/Request/Target/Object/Time/System), ClosedField (48 typed variants enumerating §9.2 base + §26.1 Wave 4 + §26.6 Wave 9 + §27.1 Wave 5 + §28.1 Wave 6 + §29.1 Wave 17, plus TargetAdapterDeclared(String) escape hatch per §9.2 'adapter-declared target fields'), Value (String/Identifier/Int/Bool/Timestamp). ClosedField::resolve(ns, subpath) closes the closed-namespace contract: unknown subpath under subject/request/object/time/system → None; unknown subpath under target → admitted as TargetAdapterDeclared. New crates/aios-policy/src/conditions_parser.rs (~700 LoC; 4 inline unit tests). pub fn parse(&str) -> Result<Condition, ConditionParseError>. Hand-written recursive-descent (no pest/nom/combine added per §21 stack lock). ConditionParseError typed enum: UnexpectedEof / UnexpectedToken / UnknownNamespace / UnknownField / UnknownOperator / DisallowedToken (or/not/parens) / UnterminatedString / InvalidInteger / EmptyValueList. Every variant carries 1-based (line, column). Bare time.recovery_mode boolean sugar per §9.1 last alternative. Determinism: parser is left-to-right single-pass, no global state, no I/O — §13.1 contract pinned by 100-iter round-trip test. EvalContext<'ctx> borrows (HydratedSubject, ActionEnvelope, EnrichmentSnapshot, ClockSnapshot — passed in for replay determinism). ConditionEvalError typed enum: TypeMismatch / UnsupportedOperator / HeterogeneousValueList. pub fn evaluate(&Condition, &EvalContext) -> Result<bool, ConditionEvalError> with left-to-right AND short-circuit on first false. resolve_field_value() maps every ClosedField to FieldValue (Str/StrList/Bool/Int/Absent). Wave 4+ subject fields + Request risk fields + target/object/system namespaces surface as Absent today (T-021/T-022 wire real lookups). target.<subpath> resolved against envelope.request.target serde_json::Value. Adapter-declared target sub-fields likewise — supports the §11.1 YAML sample rule (target.service in ["nginx", ...]). discovered_constraints_T015: CompactionBackend trait is intentionally synchronous; both backends' impls bridge to tokio-locked state via blocking_write()/blocking_read(). Production drives the worker from a spawn_blocking task; the integration test uses tokio::task::spawn_blocking. Documented in compaction.rs module header.

compact_segment MUST preserve the sealed-segment metadata row in the segments CF — the chain witness (seal hash + Ed25519 signature) is what keeps the cross-segment chain verifiable on reopen. Compaction state lives in a SEPARATE metadata CF sentinel `compacted_at::<segment_id>` so the segment-row byte layout is byte-identical to its pre-compaction form (§11.4 cryptographic recovery is untouched).

list_sealed_segments in both backends filters out already-compacted segments — otherwise OperatorApproval mode would re-emit COMPACTION_APPROVAL_REQUIRED every tick for already-compacted segments (operator UX disaster + chain pollution). The in-memory backend uses warm_receipt_count > 0 as the predicate; the RocksDB backend uses rocksdb_segment_compacted_at(...).is_none().

RecordType vocabulary expansion (Wave 13 dense 1..=427 + Wave 14+ reserved 1000..=9999) required splitting the wire_id_ranges test into two: wave_13_id_range_is_contiguous_and_dense (asserts 1..=427 stays dense) + wave_14_plus_ids_live_in_the_reserved_block (asserts every id > 427 is in 1000..=9999). The TOTAL_RECORD_TYPE_VARIANTS = WAVE_13_VARIANTS + WAVE_14_PLUS_VARIANTS bookkeeping pattern is the constitutional anchor for future Wave 14+ additions.

Proto3 reserved-range tightening: changed `reserved 1000 to 9999` to `COMPACTION_APPROVAL_REQUIRED = 1000` + `reserved 1001 to 9999`. The reserved-range overlap with a defined value is a proto3 compile error; this split is the standard pattern.

Auto-mode test against InMemoryEvidenceLog::seal_current_segment(retention_class) — note that the open-chain receipts (3 segments × N receipts) are sealed INTO three SealedSegment envelopes which carry their own retention_class. The CompactionWorker then sees one FOREVER segment + two STANDARD_24M segments; FOREVER never compacts regardless of wall-clock (is_compaction_eligible enforces this at the eligibility predicate, not at the policy level).

Sealed segment receipts() returns the original appended receipts PLUS the terminal SEGMENT_SEALED witness minted by Segment::seal(). The witness is NOT in the receipts CF (it's only in the SealedSegment envelope on the segments CF). For RocksDB compact_segment, the WriteBatch iterates ALL receipts in the envelope including the witness — the witness's delete is a no-op on the receipts CF but the count returned reflects the iteration. Test assertions skip the witness when verifying ReadReceipt round-trips against appended receipts.

Worker is purely sync + holds no Arc<Backend> reference. Tests construct one InMemoryEvidenceLog::clone() into the spawn_blocking closure and call tick(&mut backend, now) directly. Backend mutations are visible across clones because the underlying sealed_segments is Arc<Mutex<...>> (shared) and the chain state is Arc<RwLock<...>> (shared).
current_step_T016: T-016 closed — aios-policy crate skeleton landed at v0.0.1 with 5 new tests (workspace 364 → 369). Crate structure: src/lib.rs + src/decision.rs (Decision 4-variant + PolicyDecision 14-field per S2.3 §4) + src/hard_deny.rs (HardDenyClass 10-variant w/ EnumIter+EnumCount, serde wire form `hd.<snake>`) + src/subject.rs (SubjectType 7-variant lowercase wire + HydratedSubject 7-field per §7) + src/constraints.rs (Constraints + ApprovalRequirement STUB structs — T-017 fills §10 11-field vocabulary) + src/error.rs (PolicyError: SubjectUnauthenticated/EnrichmentUnavailable/BundleLoad{reason}/SchemaInvalid{detail}). tests/types_roundtrip.rs: 5 tests anchor Decision active=3 + Unspecified sentinel, HardDenyClass::COUNT==10 invariant, PolicyDecision serde round-trip, HydratedSubject serde round-trip, PolicyError Display strings. Cross-crate type linkage anchored: PolicyDecision.action_id is aios_action::ActionId; PolicyDecision.evaluated_at is chrono::DateTime<Utc>. Cargo.toml workspace members updated (aios-policy added). All 4 gates PASS: cargo check + test (369) + clippy --all-targets -D warnings + fmt --check. Scope guard held: no PolicyKernel trait, no EvaluatePolicy, no gRPC, no bundle loading — all deferred to T-017+.
discovered_constraints_T016: Workspace forbids clippy::expect_used + clippy::panic; integration tests must opt-in with `#![allow(clippy::expect_used, clippy::panic, reason = ...)]` per the established aios-action pattern. Skipped this initially and clippy gate failed loudly with 14 errors before fix.
clippy::doc_markdown is enforced at workspace level; any bare crate identifier in doc-comments (`serde_json`, `PascalCase`, etc.) must be backticked. Caught two violations on first clippy pass.

Decision enum uses #[serde(rename_all = SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE)] so the wire form matches the proto3 IDL exactly (ALLOW / REQUIRE_APPROVAL / DENY / UNSPECIFIED). The Unspecified variant is the proto3 zero-value sentinel; it never participates in real evaluation per the no-silent-fall-through rule of S2.3 §3.

HardDenyClass serde wire form uses #[serde(rename = "hd.<snake>")] so external bundle YAML/proto encoding uses the constitutional spec keys, not the Rust PascalCase variant names. EnumIter + EnumCount derived from strum_macros (already in workspace) gives the COUNT == 10 compile-time invariant anchor.
notes: M3 opened with T-016 (aios-policy crate skeleton). 369 workspace tests. Next task T-017 candidate scope: PolicyKernel trait + EvaluatePolicy signature + bundle loading skeleton (signed YAML reader, version pinning, schema validation entry points). Conditions parser (S2.3 §9) and full Constraints vocabulary (S2.3 §10) likely T-018 / T-019.
